entry.title: SAP Business AI in Automotive - Partner live expert session
entry.content:
AI is reshaping Automotive.  Fast.  The question is, are you ready to lead that conversation?

Join our live expert session and get straight to what matters:
• Key Automotive trends
• Real SAP Business AI use cases
• How to position and sell AI with confidence
• Live demo on SDE 2.0
• Open Q&A to sharpen your approach

Honestly, this isn’t just another webinar.  It’s a shortcut to turning AI into real pipeline.

Because knowing AI is good.  Selling it?  That’s better.

Register now and get in the driver’s seat.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/mar/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013389.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SalesEnablement #AITransformation #AutomotiveAI

entry.title: Terraform in Practice: Setting Up Joule Studio
entry.content:
Introduction 

As Developer Advocates, we run SAP CodeJam events where participants need hands-on access to the Joule Studio environment, typically provided by us during the day of the event. The challenge: a Joule Studio tenant has limits on the number of deployments that can be made, and these limits are reached in a CodeJam setting where 30+ developers are simultaneously testing their skills and agents, pushing deployments etc, so each CodeJam event requires spinning up a brand new tenant from scratch knowing which subscription, instances, plan, role collections, destinations, and credentials to use while creating a new tenant. 

Doing these tasks by hand is a long and tedious process. It takes too many clicks and careful typing, which makes it easy to make a mistake. With CodeJam events happening frequently, this process was becoming a real bottleneck. Terraform seemed like a good choice at this point to perform these steps. Instead of repeating the same manual steps every time, we could automate the entire tenant provisioning process, turning what used to take significant manual effort into a few commands and getting a Joule Studio tenant at the end of the execution which typically takes 20 to 30 mins. 

I had the opportunity to explore Terraform during a hands-on session at SAP Inside Track, Bangalore in June 2025.  After trying it out in the developer hands on area, I could see its value for automating the creation and management of SAP BTP subaccounts. During Devtoberfest 2025, SAP experts on Terraform presented a session titled From Clicks to Code: Automate SAP BTP Setup with Terraform on how to set up a BTP environment with Terraform. While trying to learn more about Terraform, I also came across a learning.sap.com course that is comphrensive. The course is available here and the GitHub repository used in this learning course is available here. There are also some excellent posts in the SAP Community portal for beginners looking to get started with Terraform, the links to those posts can be found in the Additional References section. Rather than repeating what's already well-documented, I'll focus on the key concepts behind the Terraform and share a few things I picked up along the way and provide you a Terraform script to get started with Joule Studio setup.  

What is Terraform? 

Terraform uses HashiCorp Configuration Language for building infrastructure with configuration files. Terraform's configuration language is declarative, meaning that it describes the desired end state for your infrastructure. It also keeps track of your real infrastructure in a state file, which acts as a source of truth for your environment. Terraform uses the state file to determine the changes to make to your infrastructure so that it will match your configuration. Terraform builds a resource graph to determine resource dependencies and creates or modifies non-dependent resources in parallel. This allows Terraform to provision resources efficiently. Terraform plugins called providers lets Terraform interact with cloud platforms and other services via their application programming interfaces (APIs). SAP provides dedicated Terraform providers for BTP, Cloud Identity, Cloud Foundry, and Cloud Connector. The BTP provider functions by wrapping BTP CLI commands, allowing you to convert imperative CLI commands into declarative Terraform resources. See the registry for documentation on how to use them: https://registry.terraform.io/search/providers?namespace=SAP. 

Using Terraform as an Administrator 

There are two ways to run the terraform script to create a Joule Studio environment. We will look at how to run the Terraform scripts on a local machine with Terraform CLI first. The approach mentioned below requires minor changes depending on whether we are running it on a Mac or Windows machine. 

  1. Clone this repository https://github.com/SAP-samples/joule-studio-setup-terraform
  2. Open it in VS Code
  3. Go through the pre-requisites section in the README file 
  4. Install Terraform CLI https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/tutorials/aws-get-started/install-cli 
  5. Set up environment variables by running these commands on your VS Code terminal  

On Mac/Linux 

export TF_VAR_btp_username='your-btp-username' 
export TF_VAR_btp_password='your-btp-user-password'

On Windows (PowerShell) 

$env:TF_VAR_btp_username = 'your-btp-username' 
$env:TF_VAR_btp_password = 'your-btp-user-password'

6. Configure the values for global account subdomain, identity provider domain, subdomain name and region in the terraform.tfvars file

Project in VS Code 7. Go to the root directory and run the commands from your terminal one by one. 

terraform init
terraform apply --auto-approve

The terraform init command installs the SAP BTP and Cloud foundry providers configured in the main.tf file in the repository.  

The terraform apply command installs the entitlements and subscriptions mentioned in the .tf files. 

8. Once all the resources are created, run Joule booster to create formation to complete the Joule studio setup. See the steps here on running the Joule booster. 

9. Navigate to the Instances and Subscriptions page of your new BTP subaccount; ensure the following subscriptions and service instances have been successfully provisioned.

The second approach is explained in the GitHub Actions section. 

GitHub Actions 

Now, the local Terraform CLI approach works perfectly fine and well suited for a development workflow, but what if you would rather not install anything on your machine at all? 

That's where GitHub Actions comes in. GitHub Actions lets you automate anything, triggered either by an event in your repository (like a push or a PR) or kicked off manually straight from the GitHub UI. Your workflows live as YAML files inside the .github/workflows directory of your repository. 

Check out the README of this repository to follow this approach for creating your Joule Studio setup: https://github.com/SAP-samples/joule-studio-setup-terraform 

One thing worth knowing: you can override any terraform.tfvars variables defined in your repository with the corresponding values in the GitHub variables by passing them via the --var flag. You can see how to pass the variables in the terraform.yml file under .github/workflows of the repository. Anything you haven't explicitly overridden will simply fall back to whatever is defined in terraform.tfvars. 

Use this approach when you have a stable version of a script that can be used to create a new tenant with all the resources and dependencies. 

Using Terraform as a Developer 

My recommendation would be to stick to the local Terraform CLI approach while you are actively writing or updating your scripts. The reason is simple: when something fails during development, Terraform lets you fix just the broken parts and apply only those changes. That kind of control is needed when you are iterating quickly. 

Before you start customizing the script to fit your needs, there are also a handful of expressions and constructs worth getting accustomed to.  So, let’s walk through the significant ones. 

Variables 

All the variables used in the script are defined in the variables.tf file with an optional default value if needed. Configurable values such as global account name, sub account region etc can be provided in the terraform.tfvars file. Sensitive information like passwords and secrets should be stored as environment variables in local machines or as GitHub secrets in GitHub. Terraform makes this easy with the TF_VAR_ prefix. Any environment variable with that prefix gets automatically picked up as a Terraform input variable. So, if you set TF_VAR_btp_password in your environment, Terraform maps it directly to var.btp_password in your script. This keeps credentials in GitHub Secrets or Environment variables and passes them securely to Terraform without exposing them in logs or files. 

If a variable has no default value and you haven't provided it through .tfvars or an environment variable, Terraform will prompt you for it in the terminal during plan or apply. It won't silently fail, but it will interrupt your flow, so it's worth getting your variables sorted before you run anything.  

Built -in functions: 

There is a set of built-in functions available that you can use within your scripts from this reference. Some of the functions used in the repository are lower(), replace(), trim(), toset(), contains() etc. When a subaccount is created, the name of the subaccount subdomain needs to confirm certain rules defined by BTP. We could use these built-in functions to sanitize the user input provided in the terraform.tfvars file and create a subaccount subdomain that confirms those naming conventions.  

raw_subdomain = lower("${var.subaccount_name}-${var.subaccount_subdomain}-${var.subaccount_region}") 
subaccount_subdomain = trim (replace (replace (raw_subdomain, "/[^a-z0-9-]/", "-"), "/--+/", "-"), "-")

This code sanitizes and formats a string to create a subdomain by concatenating three variables into lowercase and using regular expressions to replace any invalid special characters or double-hyphens with a single dash.

Meta-argument 

depends_on is a meta-argument to define explicit dependency. For eg: if you want to create a subscription for Build Work Zone but the entitlement for Build Work Zone should exist before the subscription happens. In such cases, the depends_on argument can be used to ensure that the entitlement is created before the subscription. By default, Terraform executes resources in parallel.

local values block  

When you want to define a local variable or derive a value from a variable, you can use locals. For instance, the cf_api_url is derived from subaccount_region variable, so we can define it within local value block and then use them by referencing it as local.cf_api.url  

locals {
  cf_api_url = https://api.cf.${var.subaccount_region}.hana.ondemand.com 
}

Ternary operators 

Ternary operators can be used to apply conditional expressions. 

plan_name = var.environment == "prod" ? "standard" : "free"

If the environment is production, it assigns the value "standard" to plan_name.

What I learned about the Development workflow 

 After working with Terraform, here is what I consider to be the best practices to follow: 

  • Always start with terraform fmt -recursive before committing anything. It ensures your code style remains consistent and identifies basic syntax issues early, preventing them from causing problems later
  • Run terraform validate after every code change. It's quick, doesn't need credentials, and gives you instant feedback on syntax and data type errors 
  • Never skip the terraform plan -out=tfplan even for "small" changes. This step shows you exactly what Terraform is about to create, modify, or destroy. Review the output carefully before moving on
  • Then apply with terraform apply tfplan using the saved plan file. This guarantees you are applying exactly what you reviewed 
  • Avoid --auto-approve flags in production
  • Never commit .tfstate files as state files can contain sensitive data like passwords and secrets. Add this file to .gitignore. Use a secure remote state backend instead 
  • Always commit .terraform.lock.hcl. This locks your provider versions and ensures everyone on the team gets reproducible results

Further extension of the script 

Think of the script as your starting point, it gets you a working Joule Studio setup quickly. Depending on your scenario, you will likely want to extend it.  

For Joule to connect to SAP AI Core (for document grounding), an S3 bucket (for storing documents), an MCP Server (for access to live data) or external OData APIs via BTP destinations, you will need to add those destinations in Terraform. Create a new destination.tf file, add your destination configurations and commit it to the repository and execute the terraform cli commands.

One other thing worth mentioning is Cloud Identity Services. The script assumes your global account already has Cloud Identity Services set up, which is the case for most existing BTP accounts. If that is your case, all you need to do is provide the identity provider domain value in terraform.tfvars and you are good to go. 

However, if your account doesn't have Cloud Identity Services configured yet in the global account, you will need to add a resource for the entitlement and subscription as well. For that, I would point you to the Cloud Identity Services provider documentation, it covers exactly what you need to set that up. 

 Refer to the samples shown here for setting up destination and Cloud Identity Services

  •  https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest/docs/resources/subaccount_destination 
  • https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/sap-cloud-identity-services/latest/docs 

 Additional References

  • https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-terraform-samples 
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/automating-sap-btp-setup-with-the-new-terraform-provider-for-sap-btp/ba-p/13549469 
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/extending-infrastructure-as-code-for-sap-btp-managing-resources-on-sap-btp/ba-p/14062455 
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/easy-way-to-automate-and-configure-the-setup-of-a-btp-account-and-cloud/ba-p/13677419

entry.title: [UPDATE] BTP Trial Update: Kyma Runtime Availability
entry.content:
Dear SAP BTP trial community,

We always aimed to give users the most freedom and flexibility in the SAP BTP trial to ensure that everyone can test and explore our software or use it for learning purposes. For the next weeks, the SAP BTP trial will not be able to deliver this experience for new SAP BTP trial accounts as we have to prioritize the security and reliability of our SAP BTP trial system. We are experiencing an unusually high amount of misuse limiting the availability for all of our users. 

Therefore, we decided to temporarily reduce the offering for the SAP BTP Kyma runtime for new BTP Trial Accounts. You will still be able to build applications and preview them, but deployment in the SAP BTP Kyma runtime will not be possible for the next weeks. 

How can you still explore SAP BTP? 

  • All other services will remain available and can be explored on the SAP Discovery Center
  • As a partner: Refer to our newly released free TDD licenses
  • As a customer: Leverage the free tier offering as part of Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP available in the SAP Store

All updates will be posted in the SAP Community. 

 

See you soon, 

SAP BTP trial product team

entry.title: AI and UX sessions at SAP Sapphire 2026 that I’m most excited about
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire is where SAP makes its big announcements about its vision for the future of ERP and business applications. This includes showing new products, presenting customers who have transformed their business using SAP’s solutions, and highlighting new technologies that will shape the future.

For 2026, AI will continue to dominate the event. AI will change the way that people consume enterprise software through chat-based interfaces and screens that generate on-the-fly based on what you want to do. Eventually.

In this post, I’ll share the sessions at Sapphire and ASUG Annual Conference that I’m most excited about. For the full menu, you can browse the session catalog. My preferences are, of course, shaped by my role on the Cloud ERP UX Foundation team. I’ll organize by date so you can plan your agenda. You can also use the browser to search by topic or speaker if that makes it easier for you. Some are even available on-demand. Let’s go!

Monday, May 11, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Monday
10am – 5pm
ASUG102

Unlocking Business Value with AI: Strategy, Security, and the Road Ahead

Gain a comprehensive and practical overview of SAP Business AI. This workshop will examine core strategies, security frameworks, and future trends while exploring real-world customer stories. The session will conclude with an expert panel and a hands-on AI road mapping session.

Many experts from SAP

Monday
2:00pm – 2:20pm
ERP2143

The future of enterprise: Innovations in SAP Cloud ERP Private

Discover how SAP Cloud ERP Private solutions enable the next era of enterprise transformation. This introductory session explores how cutting-edge artificial intelligence, trusted data, and integrated applications work in harmony, creating smarter, faster, and more resilient businesses within the security and flexibility of your private cloud.

Nicole Smythe, SAP

Vinay V, SAP

Monday
2:30pm – 2:50pm
CXP2149

Legacy to legendary: The AI-enhanced path

Explore the strategic road map for moving from legacy on-premises systems to a cloud-native, AI-enhanced architecture. Discover how a clean core approach helps reduce technical debt, accelerate innovation, and position your organization to use AI for greater efficiency and agility.

 

Monday
3:30pm – 3:50pm
PUX1349

Innovations and value that SAP Business AI offers

Learn how you can find the best AI features for your use cases, and understand the business value and associated cost with our self-service tools.

Pascal Mauge, SAP

 

Tuesday, May 12, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Tuesday
10:30 am - 11:15 am PUX1228

Creating better user experiences with AI-assisted SAP Fiori apps | PUX1228

New AI features in SAP Fiori apps boost user productivity and speed onboarding. Starting with the “My Home” page and drilling down into typical SAP Fiori apps, see how AI makes it easier to find information, enter data, automate tasks, and make informed business decisions. Explore how conversational interactions make SAP solutions more intuitive.

 

Peter Spielvogel, SAP

Tuesday
11:00 am - 11:20 am PUX1346

Great features don’t guarantee success: Experience does

Explore SAP Business Suite in the cloud to discover how you can shape the experience of the product for stellar adoption success.

Daniel Becker, SAP

Tuesday
11:30 am - 12:15 pm FOU1861

ASUG Community Conversation: Artificial intelligence

Explore platform strategy, embedded and generative AI use cases, integration patterns, and road map priorities with experts in AI and SAP Business Technology Platform. Discuss architecture, data readiness, and clean core alignment while exchanging lessons learned with peers advancing AI innovation.

John Astill, SAP

Tuesday
11:30 am - 12:15 pm ERP1622

SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA: What’s new, why change, and why now?

 Learn from more than 2,400 cloud ERP projects as we navigate the shift from SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ECC) to SAP S/4HANA. Compare greenfield, brownfield, and selective data transitions to find the right path. Discover the key differences in SAP S/4HANA, the value of moving now, and how to accelerate AI adoption with modern technology.

 

Esther Vrieze, SAP

Bert Schulze, SAP

Tuesday
11:30 am - 12:15 pm BTM2345

Why ERP transformations fail: The change management gap

Organizations expect operational efficiency and digital capabilities from their SAP solution transformations, yet some ERP programs fail to realize full value. The missing link is often adoption. See how integrating organizational change management early in the SAP lifecycle reduces resistance, accelerates adoption, and improves business outcomes.

 

Kara Beck, Moog Inc.

Tuesday
1:00 pm - 1:45 pm ERP1864

ASUG Community Conversation: Cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA

In this interactive ASUG Community Conversation for professionals in cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA, explore strategies across migration, operation, and innovation—featuring our road maps, clean core and AI insights, and customer lessons learned. Exchange practical guidance with peers and shape future discussions through open dialogue.

Peter Spielvogel, SAP

Nicole Smythe, SAP

Tuesday
1:00 pm - 1:20 pm JOU2645

The future of enterprise applications and AI experiences

Unlock real value from agentic AI with insights from SAP experts and peers. Explore what transformation means today and how you can scale AI across the business, overcome common challenges, govern risk, and build an AI-first enterprise that drives measurable top- and bottom-line impact.

 

Tuesday
2:00 pm - 2:45 pm ERP1178

2026 and beyond: Discover the latest innovations in SAP Cloud ERP Private 

Running your ERP in the cloud allows you to benefit from innovations at your own pace. Explore recent AI and other functional developments planned for the next releases of SAP Cloud ERP Private solutions to help you prepare for adoption, build your road map, and accelerate time to value.

Maura Hameroff, SAP

Rudolf Hois, SAP

Tuesday
3:00 pm - 3:45 pm FOU1559

Build enterprise-grade pro-code agents on SAP Business Technology Platform

Unlock the power of autonomous agents with enterprise-grade guarantees. Get technical tips on building pro-code agents on SAP Business Technology Platform that balance innovation with responsibility. Learn to use the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for smooth tool integration while supporting security, ethical AI behavior, and regulatory compliance.

Evgenii Skrebtcov, SAP

Tim Back, SAP

Tuesday
3:00 pm - 3:45 pm ERP1834

Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while keeping a clean core | ERP1834

Extend the capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Cloud to address your business needs. Use AI to generate projects in the ABAP programming language and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model from your business requirements. Build SAP Fiori apps based on your written or graphical description, and use natural language filtering to find information in them.

Katja Zoch, SAP

Tuesday
3:30 pm - 3:50 pm JOU2646

AI as the new UI

The interface is disappearing, and that's the point. As AI becomes the primary way people interact with enterprise software, the question is no longer how to design better screens but how to deliver better outcomes. Intent replaces navigation, conversation replaces configuration, and the most powerful user experience is the one you barely notice.

 

Tuesday
4:00 pm - 4:20 pm PUX1192

SAP's user experience strategy for an AI-powered web and mobile future

Get an overview of how SAP is helping business users get their work done more easily and with better outcomes: using the Joule solution and embedded AI to take UX to the next level, providing a consistent and integrated suite experience, and leveraging our user research framework to meet users' needs. Also see how you can create great UX yourself.

Arin Bhowmick, SAP

 

Wednesday, May 13, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Wednesday
10:30 am – 11:15 am SAP1623

SAP ECC to SAP Business Suite: Migration strategies and cloud insights

Master the direct transition from SAP ERP Central Component to SAP Business Suite using proven best practices. Compare public, private, and on-premises deployments to define key decision drivers. Learn to navigate annual releases, leverage essential migration tools, and identify the evolving skills needed for a successful, seamless cloud journey.

Esther Vrieze, SAP

Bert Schultze, SAP

Wednesday
10:30 am – 11:15 am PUX1819

SAP Fiori in action: How modern UX drives efficiency in cloud ERP

Get an overview of how the SAP Fiori user experience (UX) helps users get their work done more easily and efficiently. See how we are using AI today in applications and with the Joule solution. Understand our road map for revolutionizing UX, how AI changes the idea of what an application is, and what all this means for you.

Thomas Reiss, SAP

Wednesday
11:30 am – 11:50 am SUS1523

AI that helps you operate your business

As sustainability shifts from reporting to real business decisions, it becomes a control and execution challenge. Explore how you can replace manual processes with AI for trusted, scalable insights and decision support, delivering accountability for operational and financial decision-making.

Sophia Mendelsohn, SAP

Wednesday
11:30 am – 12:15 pm PUX1195

Creating AI-powered UX for your own applications with SAP Design System

Learn how to create AI-powered UX for your applications by using SAP Design System (SAP Fiori UX for web and mobile apps) with its powerful set of reusable components, patterns, and floorplans. See how building applications is made easier with our AI-enabled UI technologies for web and mobile apps.

Sophia Levens, SAP

Frederic Berg, SAP

Wednesday
11:30 am – 12:15 pm ERP2238

Western Sugar’s success with SAP Cloud ERP

Five years after go-live, Western Sugar Cooperative calls SAP Cloud ERP applications “wonderful.” Hear about why it chose public cloud; the value of clean core, best practices, strong configuration and integration, and continuous improvement; its smooth move from a two- to three-system landscape; and readiness for AI-enabled innovation.

 

Wednesday
11:30 am – 12:15 pm FOU2337

When AI becomes the interface to the enterprise

For decades, SAP helped shape enterprise software based on screens and transactions. Today, AI is evolving how we interact with systems. Explore how SAP leads this shift—uniting applications, data, and AI—and what this next phase of transformation means for organizations turning AI into a real competitive advantage.

Florian Heretsch, SAP

Wednesday
1:00 pm – 1:45 pm FOU1557

Getting started with SAP Build MCPs: Create your first CAP app and process

 Experience the powerful Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration in SAP Build solutions. Produce your first SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) application and SAP Fiori elements UI, while orchestrating workflows. See how AI-enhanced context improves coding efficiency and helps you build scalable, process-rich apps.

 

Tim Back, SAP

Jason Gabor, SAP

Wednesday
1:00 pm – 1:45 pm BTM1232

The AI-augmented architect: Upgrading architecture in the age of AI 

 AI is reshaping enterprise architecture. To provide value, enterprise architectures must embrace AI tools. Discover SAP technology leveraging AI for enhanced visibility and coordination. Through real-world examples, see how Joule and agentic AI increase information velocity and augment architect capabilities to thrive in this evolving landscape.

Martin Mysyk, SAP

Wednesday
2:00 pm – 2:20 pm PUX1210

AI and the future of user experience in SAP software

Prepare yourself for the upcoming revolution in user experience by understanding how we are harnessing AI to help users achieve better outcomes. Discover how AI is changing the idea of what an application is—and what this means for you. Learn about our AI design patterns and get a glimpse of the future SAP software user experience.

Emil Voutta, SAP

Wednesday
2:00 pm – 2:20 pm JOU2647

The future of enterprise applications and AI experiences

The enterprise application is no longer a system of record but a system of action. As AI moves from assistant to agent, organizations shift from what their software can store to what it can do. Learn why the future belongs to applications that don't just surface information but understand context, anticipate decisions, and act with confidence.

 

Wednesday
3:00 pm – 3:20 pm PUX1194

User experience innovations and road map for SAP Business Suite

Have a look at the latest user experience innovations across our suite of applications, along with exciting web and mobile innovations coming soon. Also check out selected highlights from Ul technologies for creating great UX for your own applications.

Tom Reiss, SAP

I’m excited to hear what we announce in the keynotes and experience the future of enterprise software. AI will transform how we all interact with apps. The future starts now.

Here are some other summaries of Sapphire sessions from other colleagues.

Inside SAP Build at SAP Sapphire 2026: What to Expect from AI Agents and Agentic Development by Mark Wright

See you in Orlando!

For the SAP Cloud ERP UX Foundation team, Peter Spielvogel.

entry.title: SAP Business AI in Wholesale Distribution - Partner live expert session
entry.content:
AI in Wholesale Distribution isn’t just a trend; it’s quickly becoming the difference between reacting and leading.

In this upcoming session, we’ll walk through how SAP Business AI is helping customers in Wholesale Distribution work smarter, respond faster, and unlock real productivity gains.  The thing is that many partners are already having these conversations… but not everyone feels fully equipped to lead them with confidence.

We’ll start with a clear view of the market and key industry themes, then go deeper into practical AI use cases (the kind your customers care about).  Plus, we’ll share how to position and sell these solutions effectively, because great tech only matters if it resonates.

And yes, there’s a live demo on SDE 2.0 to bring it all together.

If you’re looking to turn AI conversations into tangible opportunities, this one’s worth your time.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/mar/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013492.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #BusinessAI #WholesaleDistribution #DigitalTransformation

entry.title: Your AI Models Just Got a Hardware Upgrade
entry.content:
Co-Author: Lukas Pätz

TL;DR

SAP AI Core (the engine) now supports flexible instance types — pick the exact CPU or GPU your workload needs
New instance types deliver up to 4.5–5x the compute performance of old resource plans
Access the latest GPUs (L4, L40S, H100) without waiting for SAP to package them into plans
Your existing resource plans still work — instance types are opt-in where they help

Start Here: SAP Note 3660109

Why This Matters

If you're running your custom AI models on SAP's Generative AI Hub (technical service SAP AI Core), this one's for you.

We've heard the feedback: newer GPUs are available from hyperscalers, but you can't use them yet because SAP hasn't released a matching resource plan. That lag is frustrating — especially when the difference between a V100 and an H100 isn't incremental. It's generational. We're talking 4.5–5x the compute performance for the right workloads. An inference call that takes 8 seconds on older hardware might finish in under 1 second on newer chips. That's the gap between "possible in theory" and "shipping in production."

Starting now, that gap is closing.
 

What Changed: Resource Plans → Instance Types

Think of the old model like a set menu at a restaurant. Curated, predictable, but limited to what the chef decided to offer.

Instance types are the shift to à la carte. You see the full range of CPUs and GPUs available across cloud providers, and you pick exactly what your workload needs — from a lightweight CPU for preprocessing to an H100 for training large models.

Resource plans aren't going away. They still work. But instance types are now the primary path for getting the newest, most capable hardware into your deployments — faster, and with finer control than the old model allowed. We highly recommend to leverage instance types going forward (only available in the "extended" service plan, now be the right time to migrate from standard to extended)

Here's the point: you tell us what your model needs; we make sure the right compute is there. Bon appétit.

What You Actually Get

A quick clarification: If you're using pre-integrated foundation models through SAP's Generative AI Hub — GPT-5.2, Claude 4.6 Opus or Gemini — this update doesn't directly affect you. Those models are already managed and optimized; you just call the API.

Instance types matter when you're running your own models: custom fine-tuned models, your proprietary models (like team-built solutions), embedding models for RAG pipelines, or specialized computer vision and industry models. That's when hardware choice becomes your concern — and when better hardware translates directly into better outcomes.

Faster inference, faster training

This isn't about spec sheets. It's about what becomes possible with the models you've built.

Custom models that were borderline viable for real-time use — domain-specific classifiers, fine-tuned embedding models, proprietary prediction models — become clearly viable when you can run them on L40S instead of T4. Training jobs that took days can finish in hours on H100. Features your team considered out of reach might now fit inside your existing budget.

L4 and L40S chips deliver substantially higher throughput than T4, at lower cost per inference call. H100 provides order-of-magnitude gains over V100 for training workloads. If you're pushing the limits of what's feasible with custom models, this matters.

Better cost efficiency — without re-architecting

Faster hardware means less compute time per job, which means lower cost. Simple as that.

Instance types also let you right-size. You stop paying for a resource plan heavier than you need. The result: better cost-per-output without touching your model or application logic.

 

New hardware when it ships — not months later

When hyperscalers release the next GPU generation, you won't be waiting for SAP to define a new resource plan. Instance types let us surface new hardware significantly faster. Your infrastructure stays current.

Multi-cloud, multi-region resilience

Instance types are available across cloud providers and regions. Deploy closer to your users or data. Meet compliance requirements. Reduce single-vendor dependency. Keep two or three validated alternatives per region and you're protected against capacity constraints.

Which Instance Should You Use?

 

WorkloadRecommended InstanceExamples
Orchestration, preprocessing, CPU-based MLCPU (AWS m7i, Azure Dv6, GCP n4)Data pipelines, API routing, lightweight model serving
Custom model inference (high throughput)L4 / L40S GPU (AWS g6, g6e)Fine-tuned classifiers, embedding models, custom vision models
Fine-tuning and training medium-to-large modelsA100 (GCP A2 Ultra)Domain-specific model fine-tuning, complex custom inference
Training large language modelsH100 (Azure NC H100 v5)Custom LLM training, proprietary foundation model development

Match the instance to your workload. Account for regional availability. Keep at least two alternatives validated per region.

Use the compute factors in SAP Note 3660109 or the SAP Cost Calculator to compare options on a consistent basis — each instance type maps to a compute factor in node-hours, which makes cost planning straightforward.

Migration: No Big Bang Required

Both options work today. You don't have to migrate everything at once. More details:

# This still works
ai.sap.com/resourcePlan: "infer.s"

# This is the new option — use where it helps
ai.sap.com/instanceType: "g6.4xlarge"

Start with the workloads where newer hardware will have the clearest impact — typically your highest-volume inference jobs or your most compute-heavy training runs. Everything else can stay on resource plans, but newer hardware might be more efficient.

Full specs, regional availability, and compute factors: SAP Note 3660109.

Staying Current

Hardware in AI is moving fast — faster than enterprise software typically keeps up. Instance types are how we keep you on the right side of that curve, without adding operational overhead to your team.

To stay informed:

  • Subscribe to SAP Note 3660109 — it's the live catalogue; new instance types show up here as they become available
  • Watch the What's New page for implementation guidance and new instance families

The Bottom Line

This isn't a small tweak. It's a deliberate shift to put more control in your hands — and to get it there faster.

We handle the complexity: evaluating new hardware, qualifying it, making it available at scale. You focus on what you're actually here to do: build AI solutions that matter.

The menu just expanded. Pick your instance — and dig in.

entry.title: Building Custom Joule Skills via RFC: Guide for SAP S/4HANA and ECC Integration
entry.content:
Introduction

In today’s digital enterprise landscape, rapid access to critical business data can significantly impact operational efficiency and customer satisfaction. SAP’s AI-powered digital assistant, Joule, offers organizations the ability to interact with their enterprise systems through natural language conversations. By extending Joule with custom skills, businesses can create tailored experiences that bring real-time data directly to their teams—without the need for complex navigation through traditional ERP interfaces.

This article provides a comprehensive technical guide for building custom Joule skills that connect to SAP S/4HANA or ECC systems via RFC (Remote Function Call). We’ll explore a practical use case and walk through the complete configuration process.

Business Scenario

Consider a common challenge faced by sales and operations teams: A customer inquires about the manufacturing progress of their order. Traditionally, the specialist would need to log into the ERP system, navigate through multiple screens, and manually locate the production order details. This process is time-consuming and interrupts the flow of customer communication.

With a custom Joule skill, this same specialist can simply ask Joule in natural language—"What’s the status of production order 12345?"—and receive immediate, formatted information directly within SAP Build Workzone. This streamlined approach enhances productivity and improves response times to customer inquiries.

 

 

Solution Architecture

The technical architecture comprises several integrated SAP components:

  • SAP Build Workzone: The user interface where specialists interact with Joule
  • Joule Studio: The development environment for designing and building custom AI skills
  • SAP Build: Used to create Action Projects that expose backend functionality
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Hosts destination configurations and orchestrates connections
  • SAP Cloud Connector: Establishes secure RFC connections between BTP and on-premise or private cloud systems
  • SAP S/4HANA / ECC: The backend ERP system containing production order data

 

Configuration Steps

Step 1: Establish RFC Connectivity

The foundation of this solution is a secure connection between your cloud and on-premise environments.

Build RFC Connection with SAP Cloud Connector:
Configure the SAP Cloud Connector to create a secure tunnel between your BTP environment and your S/4HANA or ECC system. This ensures that backend functions can be accessed securely without exposing your entire system to the internet.

 

Setup Destination in BTP Cockpit:
Create a destination in your BTP subaccount that references the RFC connection established through the Cloud Connector. This destination will be consumed by SAP Build to enable communication with your backend system.

 

Step 2: Create a BAPI/RFC Action Project

With connectivity established, the next step is to expose the specific backend functionality that Joule will use.

Create the Action Project:
Navigate to the SAP Build cockpit and create a new BAPI/RFC Action project. Search for the BAPI that retrieves production order information—for example, BAPIs related to production order management.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Test the Connection:
After creating the action project, test the connection to verify that data can be successfully retrieved from your ERP system. By default, these actions operate in READ ONLY mode to protect your business data from unintended modifications.

 

 

Optional Verification with SE37:
For deeper technical validation, you can use transaction code SE37 in your S/4HANA or ECC system to directly test the BAPI function with various parameters. This helps you understand the data structures and responses before integrating them into your Joule skill.

 

 

Step 3: Design Skills in Joule Studio

This is where you design the conversational experience and connect it to your backend data.

Skill 1: Get Production Orders

Each skill consists of three major components:

  1. Skill Description: Define the purpose of the skill and the natural language triggers that will activate it when users interact with Joule.
  2. BAPI Action: Link the skill to the BAPI/RFC Action project you created in SAP Build, establishing the connection between the conversational interface and your backend data.
  3. Send Message: Design the end-user output using the Message Configuration editor. This allows you to transform raw BAPI data into formatted, interactive cards that present information clearly and professionally.

 

 

 

Skill 2: Get Production Order Detail

Following the same pattern, create a second skill that provides detailed information about a specific production order. This allows users to drill down from a list view to detailed information through natural conversation.

 

 

 

Step 4: Deploy and Enable in Workzone

The final phase makes your custom skills accessible to end users.

Create a Shared Environment:
In Joule Studio, create a Shared environment that will serve as the runtime for your deployed skills.

 

 

Deploy Your Joule Project:
Deploy your Joule project to the Shared environment. Once deployed, verify that the project appears under Custom Capabilities.

 

 

Configure Joule in SAP Build Workzone:
Set up the integration between Joule and your SAP Build Workzone site. After completing the integration configuration, enable Joule within your specific Workzone site.

 

 

Test Your Custom Capabilities:
Access your Workzone site, open the Joule client, and test your new custom AI capabilities by asking questions in natural language about production orders.

 

 

Benefits and Outcomes

By implementing custom Joule skills, organizations can achieve:

  • Increased Productivity: Eliminate time-consuming navigation through complex ERP interfaces
  • Improved Customer Service: Enable faster, more accurate responses to customer inquiries
  • Enhanced User Experience: Provide intuitive, conversational access to critical business data
  • Secure Data Access: Maintain enterprise-grade security while democratizing data access
  • Reduced Training Requirements: Lower the learning curve for accessing ERP information

Conclusion

Building custom Joule skills via RFC represents a powerful approach to modernizing how users interact with SAP systems. By combining the conversational AI capabilities of Joule with the deep business logic in S/4HANA or ECC, organizations can create intelligent, context-aware experiences that transform daily operations.

The technical implementation, while requiring careful configuration across multiple components, provides a robust and secure foundation for extending SAP’s AI capabilities to meet specific business needs. As organizations continue their digital transformation journeys, solutions like these demonstrate how AI can be practically applied to solve real business challenges.

Additional Resources

  • Build Your First Joule Skill in Joule Studio
  • Joule Studio CodeJam
  • Build custom Joule skills for SAP and non-SAP systems using Joule Studio

entry.title: VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026: Champions Crowned at SYNC DemoJam
entry.content:
The Stage is Set
The VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026 reached its spectacular grand finale on the main stage of VNSG SYNC 2026 – the annual VNSG and SAP Netherlands year event. In front of a packed keynote audience on 12 March, three finalist teams had exactly five minutes to demonstrate the business impact of their SAP BTP-powered innovations. The audience then cast their votes – and the results were electric.

From Challenge to Champion: The Journey
The hackathon launched in late 2025 when VNSG, SAP BTP Product Management and SAP NL Presales invited Dutch SAP customers and partners to submit real-life business challenges. Six teams were selected and, after a structured enablement phase, spent weeks co-building solutions with dedicated coaches. In February 2026, all six teams pitched to a jury panel, which selected three finalists for the grand DemoJam finale at SYNC. The three finalists stood out for their clear business relevance, technical ambition and the real-world potential to take their prototypes to production.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Champion: Promocean + SOA People — Purchase Order Control Center
Luc van Elk and Thomas Langhout from Promocean, supported by SOA People, took the audience vote and claimed the top spot. Their solution – the Purchase Order Control Center – puts SAP Business AI at the heart of procurement operations.
By connecting AI directly to SAP ECC, the team demonstrated how intelligent automation can dramatically reduce manual effort, accelerate purchase order processing, and surface exceptions before they become costly problems. A showcase of how AI on SAP BTP can deliver operational excellence in one of the most common – and pain-prone – business processes in any enterprise.

Runner-up: Ecotone + Expertum — Integration Suite Management
Arjan de Mol and Bart van de Kamp from Ecotone, partnered with Expertum Consulting, earned second place with a solution that tackles a pain many integration teams know all too well: managing and controlling large numbers of iFlows across landscapes. Their Integration Suite Management solution brings Joule-powered automation to iFlow lifecycle management – enabling operators to undeploy and deploy groups of iFlows intelligently. This turns a time-consuming, error-prone manual process into a governed, AI-assisted workflow, giving integration teams transparency, stability and control. 

Third Place: NTT DATA Business Solutions — ProTrack
Laurens Steffers and Jorrit van der Sanden from NTT DATA Business Solutions presented ProTrack – a solution delivering real-time insight for machines on the production floor. Built on SAP BTP, ProTrack addresses a critical operational need in manufacturing: knowing what is happening on your shop floor right now, not minutes later. By streaming machine data into an SAP BTP-powered dashboard, teams can react faster to exceptions, reduce downtime and optimize throughput.

 

Beyond the Podium: A Strong Field
The jury had a hard job. Aarini Consulting, Nederlandse Spoorwegen (NS), and Simac IT & BSC also built
impressive solutions that demonstrated real business value on SAP BTP. Every team that reached the jury pitch proved
that you can go from business challenge to working prototype in a short time - when you combine the right platform
with the right people. A sincere congratulations to all six participating teams !!

Behind the Scenes
Want to experience the atmosphere of the DemoJam from backstage? Watch the behind-the-scenes video - including a
live demo of how SAP Business AI is changing the way people interact with SAP, presented by Jason Enti and Ronald
Schippers: Behind the scenes at the VNSG SAP BTP DemoJam SYNC 2026

The People Who Made it Happen
A hackathon like this does not happen by itself. A huge thank you to everyone who contributed:
Presented at SYNC by: Twan van den Broek and Irene Rompa
Team coaches: Jan Penninkhof , Alex van den Hoek and Twan van den Broek
Jury: Ronald Schippersm Steven Nuyts, Steffen Schad, Jonathan Freeland
Made possible by: Mientje Paais, Olaf Winkler, Judy Bui and Niklas Siemer

What This Proves
The VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026 is proof that innovation does not require a blank slate or a greenfield project. It requires the right platform, the right people and a real problem worth solving.
SAP BTP – combining SAP Integration Suite, SAP Build, SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Business AI and cloud-native runtimes – gives teams everything they need to move from prototype to production at speed.
The finalist solutions were not demos for the sake of demos. They addressed real procurement pain, real integration
complexity and real manufacturing visibility gaps. And they were built in weeks, not months.

What's Next?
The conversation does not end on the SYNC stage. All teams are encouraged to work with their coaches to plan production rollouts and turn hackathon prototypes into enterprise solutions.

For the broader SAP Community : Stay tuned for the next edition of the VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon. Bring your own
real-life business challenge and co-build with partners and SAP experts.
And who knows - your solution could be on the SYNC main stage in 2027.

SAP Business Technology Platform SAP Build SAP Integration Suite SAP Business AI 

@hansvp @qmrjvd @sricsi98 @BvE @mdschoenmakers @tamasszirtes @f_van_leeuwen @Petra1 @BartvdKamp @tedcastelijns @Arjan_deMol @dvvelzen @JdTeuling @AnnikaHeus @LaurensSteffers

Related reading:
When Innovation Meets Real Business Impact – VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026
Grand Finale VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon in a DemoJam

entry.title: Clean Core for Utilities: The Missing Link Between S/4HANA and AI Driven Operations
entry.content:
Clean Core for Utilities: The Missing Link Between S/4HANA and AI‑Driven Operations

 

Most of the Utilities organization are entering one of the most disruptive decades the industry has ever seen. Aging assets due to modern technologies, rising customer expectations due to digital world, and the rapid growth of renewables are forcing companies to rethink how they operate and manage their system. Many utilities organizations have already started their S/4HANA programs with the hope that modernization will naturally lead to automation, intelligence, and AI-driven insights.

But here’s the reality most teams discover the hard way:

AI doesn’t scale on a messy foundation. And without a Clean Core, S/4HANA alone won’t get you there.

Across utilities in the U.S., APAC, Europe and the Middle East, the organizations making real progress but having  one thing in common: they aren’t just migrating — they’re rebuilding their architectural backbone from scratch of the system or existing design so AI can deliver business value the way they want it to get modern success.

Why Utilities Are Still Struggling

Many utilities organizations are carrying 15–25 years of technical debt. Their ECC  or R3 and IS‑U systems often include:

  • Custom code buried in user exits, BADI, FQ Event and enhancements both implicit and explicit.
  • Hard‑coded logic only a developer or few team member understand.
  • Manual steps scattered across the meter‑to‑cash cycle.
  • Data spread across GIS, AMI, OMS, CIS and legacy and other third-party tools.

The result is a fragmented landscape where every process behaves differently. And that’s the real blocker. Many utilities think their challenge is adopting AI. But the truth is:

AI cannot operate on inconsistent data or unpredictable processes.

What Clean Core Actually Means         

This important point is most of the people misconception that Clean Core means “no customization.” That’s not realistic for utilities, at least for now — regulatory and market needs are too complex, and greater flexibility is still needed, but future may change for above statement is true for now.

Clean Core is not about eliminating logic. It’s about putting logic in the right place.

A practical Clean Core approach means:

1. Keep SAP S/4HANA Utilities standard

Use it as a stable, upgrade‑safe system of record for metering, billing, finance, and core operations.

2. Move differentiation to SAP BTP

Build workflows, extensions, and apps on BTP — not inside the core.

3. Use events instead of modifications

A meter read posted in SAP S/4HANA Utilities should trigger an event. That event can activate workflows, validations, or AI agents on BTP — without touching billing logic.

Clean Core doesn’t slow innovation. It creates the conditions for innovation to scale safely.

Why Clean Core Is the Missing Link for AI

AI only works well when three things are true:

  • Data is consistent, this must have.
  • Processes are predictable, of course this is something to understandable.
  • System behavior is stable; this is bottleneck as well.

Clean Core enforces all three.

Without it, AI stays stuck in pilots’ phase. With it, AI becomes operational. Utilities that adopt Clean Core unlock outcomes which are:

  • Predictive billing to reduce customer call and dispute resolution.
  • Autonomous collections based on customer behavior. Important for utility industries.
  • DER/EV readiness without breaking billing logic, of course main source of revenue generation.
  • Revenue integrity through real‑time anomaly detection.

This shifts from reactive operations to predictive and autonomous ones and trust me this is future of utilities at least from my prospective with AI Era and digital world we are moving in.

Reference Architecture for an AI‑Ready Utility

This future architecture can be understood in three layers:

1.       Transactional Core (SAP S/4HANA Utilities)
  • System of record for metering, billing, and finance data
  • Standardized and upgrade‑friendly
  • Minimal custom code
2.       Innovation Layer (SAP BTP)

Where differentiation lives. Using:

  • Integration Suite
  • Build Process Automation
  • Event Mesh
  • AI Foundation
  • Joule Agents
  • Customer microservices

This is where utilities build intelligent workflows and automation — without touching the core.

3.       AI‑Driven Operations

The outcome layer:

  • Predictive insights
  • Automated processes
  • AI agents managing meter‑to‑cash
  • Intelligent customer interactions
  • Real‑time decisioning

This isn’t just a tech stack — it’s a new operating model.

A Practical Maturity Path: Utilities don’t need to jump to AI overnight. A realistic path looks like which I feel should be:

  • Level 1: Customized Core (Reactive): Heavy custom code, manual processes, limited visibility.
  • Level 2: Clean Core + BTP (Responsive): Standard core with side‑by‑side innovation.
  • Level 3: Event‑Driven + AI (Autonomous): Real‑time processes with AI‑driven decisioning.

The goal isn’t perfection — it’s steady movement toward a scalable, intelligent architecture.

 

 

How to Get Started

Here is practical first steps:

  1. Assess your landscape, identify custom code that can be retired or moved out of the core.
  2. Adopt standard processes first S/4HANA already solves many historical customizations.
  3. Build side‑by‑side by default innovation belongs on BTP.
  4. Prioritize data quality AI is only as good as the data feeding it.

Conclusion

Clean Core is no longer an architectural preference — it’s a prerequisite for AI‑driven utilities.

The future utility isn’t just delivering power or water or gas. It’s managing data, events, and intelligent processes in real time.

Utilities that recognize this shift early won’t just modernize — they’ll redefine how the industry operates.

entry.title: SAP Developer News for April 2nd, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

AI Developer Challenge April 2026 - build AI Agents with Generative AI Hub

  • Repository: https://github.com/noravth/developer-challenge-042026/blob/main/README.md
  • Blog Post: AI Developer Challenge April 2026 - build AI Agent... - SAP Community

What’s  New in SAP HANA Cloud 2026/Q1

  • SAP HANA Cloud Data & Discovery Agents - Beta Program: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/4199
  • What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – March 2026 [blog post]: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-hana-cloud-march-2026/ba-p/14355960
  • Deep Dive with Product Experts [video]: https://youtu.be/pC1o1xRwLto
  • SAP CodeJam Brazil AI Tour: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-roadshow-2026-brazil-edition-kicking-off-in-march-and/ba-p/14317926
  • HANA TechCon 2026: https://hanatech.community/

Removal of the experimental sap.ui.webc.* libraries for Web Components in UI5

  • Removal of legacy sap.ui.webc.* Web Component UI libraries from SAPUI5/OpenUI5 1.147.0 onwards (blog post) https://community.sap.com/t5/frontend-ui5-sap-fiori-blog-posts/removal-of-legacy-sap-ui-webc-web-component-ui-libraries-from-sapui5/ba-p/14358564

DYK #10 – Test Orchestration Configuration

  • More information: https://help.sap.com/docs/ai-launchpad/sap-ai-launchpad/test-your-orchestration-workflow?q=orchestration+workflow+ai

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 AI Developer Challenge April 2026 - build AI Agents with Generative AI Hub

1:10 What’s  New in SAP HANA Cloud 2026/Q1

2:37 Removal of the experimental sap.ui.webc.* libraries for Web Components in UI5

3:58 DYK #10 Test Orchestration Configuration

Transcription

00;00;00;18 - 00;00;06;04
Announcer Guy
This is the SAP Developer News for April 2nd, 2026.

00;00;06;07 - 00;00;09;26
Nora von Thenen
Challenge is coming to an end. That means that we will start a new developer

00;00;09;28 - 00;00;27;19
Nora von Thenen
challenge in April, next Monday, and that will be on the topic of I am super excited because in the first week, you will learn how to use the orchestration service and the grounding module all in Gen AI Hub on SAP AI Core. In the second week you will learn how to build a code based agent using CrewAI and Generative AI hub.

00;00;27;25 - 00;00;43;09
Nora von Thenen
And in the third week, you will learn how to actually hand over the grounding module of the orchestration service as a tool to your agent to build a simple agentic RAG workflow. And then in the last week, you will check out the Rapid-1 model and you will actually have a free system that you can try everything out on.

00;00;43;12 - 00;01;01;23
Nora von Thenen
So make sure you click on the blog post URL that is linked in the show notes, and then in there you will find the link to get your own uses assigned to you, and then you can immediately try it out. You will have access to Jupyter notebooks. We will be using the cloud SDK for AI Python version, but you can actually also, build a JavaScript version yourself on top of it.

00;01;01;23 - 00;01;07;27
Nora von Thenen
You will have examples for Python. The. Yeah. So let me know what you think and try it out.

00;01;08;00 - 00;01;34;26
Witalij Rudnicki
It is already April which means registration for the beta testing of the data and discovery agent procedures in SAP Hana Cloud has started. This agent procedures combined knowledge graph based discovery with AI driven query generation, reducing the need for deep SQL expertise when working with complex data models. This agent procedures became available with the release of the Q1 version of SAP Hana Cloud, which brought more interesting stuff for developers.

00;01;34;26 - 00;02;03;19
Witalij Rudnicki
Data professionals, and data scientists to work with. You will find detailed explanations in the blog posts and videos from product management. Among this new capabilities, which I'm most interested, in and excited about. The use of calculation views in SAP Business Data Cloud, new Gen AI powered actions in the SQL console like run prompt, explain the SQL statements and optimize SQL statements using AI.

00;02;03;25 - 00;02;27;28
Witalij Rudnicki
The performance analyzer, a new capability for analyzing historical performance data files give Hana Cloud and new features across the predictive analytics library and the Natural Language Processing or NLP services. Rest assured that this new Pal and NLP features will be incorporated into the content of this SAP CodeJams, which Nora and I will deliver starting next Friday in Brazil.

00;02;28;05 - 00;02;36;27
Witalij Rudnicki
During our AI tour, the and then later during The HANA TechCon in St. Leon Rot in July.

00;02;36;29 - 00;03;13;24
Mr. Terminal (DJ Adams)
Web components are a big deal for frontend frameworks. The UI5 framework that's both a SAPUI5 and OpenUI5 has had support for web components since 2021, in the form of the experimental SAP UI web C libraries. These libraries were introduced in 192, deprecated in one 120, and will be removed in version one. 147 zero. Note that these libraries never left experimental status.

00;03;13;26 - 00;03;45;15
Mr. Terminal (DJ Adams)
Non experimental support for web components has been available in UI5 since one. 138. The support is native, built directly into SAPUI core and much better approach. UI5’s chief Architect, Peter Muessig’s blogpost has lots of information about this. And perhaps most importantly, there's a section on what you should do, especially if you've used the experimental sap UI web C libraries in production.

00;03;45;15 - 00;03;59;29
Mr. Terminal (DJ Adams)
Despite the experimental status. Check out all the details in Peter's blog post link in the description.

00;04;00;03 - 00;04;23;15
Kevin Riedelsheimer
Did you know that you can test the orchestration configuration directly in SAP AI Launchpad against various LLMs? This allows you to run user prompts against your configuration and jump right back into the configuration view to adapt it at any point. The testing feature, even allows you to inspect and trace to get a better understanding on what your configuration impacts are.

00;04;23;16 - 00;04;33;29
Kevin Riedelsheimer
On the chat completion of the LLM. This gives you a perfect workflow for creating great orchestration flows for your chat completion and your application.
